raw_comment: In this graph, it is measuring the percentage of people ages 18-34 that don’t live with a parent who say they reach out to a parent at least once a day or a few times a week and is comparing the percentages of young men versus young women and the methods in which they communicate with their parents. I noticed that, in general, young women had a higher percentage of reaching out to parents than young men did. I also noticed that, for both women and men, the most popular means of communication was by sending texts and the least popular means of communication was visiting in person. I wonder if this is because sending texts requires much less time and effort compared to visiting in person so it is easier for young adults to quickly send a text amidst their busy daily schedules. I also noticed that there was the lowest difference in percentage between men and women for visiting in person and wonder why this is the case compared to the disparity between genders being relatively higher for phone/video-chat and sending texts. This relates to my community because I know some people in college who don’t live with their parents right now and it is often difficult for them to find time to contact their families but try to text or call them as much as they can. My headline for this graph would be: “Connecting Back Home - Is Contacting Parents a Priority?”.
ace_sentences: This graph measures a percentage.
The people are people ages 18-34.
The people do not live with a parent.
The people say that they reach out to a parent at least once a day or a few times a week.
This graph compares the percentages of young men and young women.
This graph compares the methods in which young men and young women communicate with their parents.
In general, young women had a higher percentage of reaching out to parents than young men did.
For women, the most popular means of communication was by sending texts.
For men, the most popular means of communication was by sending texts.
For women, the least popular means of communication was visiting in person.
For men, the least popular means of communication was visiting in person.
Sending texts requires much less time and effort compared to visiting in person.
It is easier for young adults to quickly send a text amidst their busy daily schedules.
There was the lowest difference in percentage between men and women for visiting in person.
The disparity between genders was relatively higher for phone/video-chat.
The disparity between genders was relatively higher for sending texts.
This relates to my community.
I know some people in college.
These people do not live with their parents right now.
It is often difficult for these people to find time to contact their families.
These people try to text their families as much as they can.
These people try to call their families as much as they can.
My headline for this graph would be: “Connecting Back Home - Is Contacting Parents a Priority?”.
